title: Redesign 'At-Risk Student Alert' — honest + supportive + legal
queue_body:
## Goal
Redesign under three hard constraints (session-scope, in-surface-only, confidence-bound) using honest reality-based language, not soft euphemism. User decision 2026-04-20: harsh-reality numbers are the target voice; euphemism is the anti-pattern.

## Hard constraints (not tone-negotiable)
1. Session-scoped only (ADR-0003) — never persisted to profile/aggregate.
2. In-surface only (RDY-080) — never emitted to parent SMS, gradebook passback, or dashboards loading after session.
3. Confidence-bounded — every number carries CI + sample size.

## Language policy (user-directed)
- Target: 'You answered 40% of mastery-threshold problems correctly today — that's below the 5-unit trajectory.'
- Banned euphemism: 'needs support', 'room to grow', 'opportunity area', 'at risk', 'concerning'.
- Ship-gate scanner enforces.

## Files
- docs/adr/NNNN-at-risk-session-surface.md
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Sessions/SessionRiskAssessment.cs (new VO: point+CI+sampleSize+generatedAt)
- tests/arch/NoAtRiskPersistenceTest.cs
- tests/arch/NoAtRiskExternalEmissionTest.cs
- src/student/full-version/src/components/session/TrajectoryIndicator.vue
- src/admin/full-version/src/views/apps/session/TeacherSessionView.vue
- AXIS_5/AXIS_6/competitive doc updates

## Definition of Done
- ADR accepted. SessionRiskAssessment lives inside session actor, absent from StudentState and any snapshot.
- Two arch tests green: no risk* field on persisted/external DTOs; no outbound adapter emits risk.
- UI renders number + CI; e2e asserts banned-euphemism list absent from trajectory component.
- Ship-gate extended with banned-euphemism scan.
- Full Cena.Actors.sln builds; all tests pass.

## Reporting
complete via: node .agentdb/kimi-queue.js complete <id> --worker claude-subagent-adr-authoring --result "<branch>"

title: Student-input UI for AdaptiveScheduler (deadline + weekly time budget)
queue_body:
## Goal
`SchedulerInputs` exposes DeadlineUtc + WeeklyTimeBudget but no UI captures them; only MotivationProfile is set (via RDY-057). Add a student-facing settings form (onboarding + editable later) that captures exam date + weekly time commitment, persists them into the student plan config aggregate, and makes them available to the scheduler at session-start time.

## Files
- src/student/full-version/src/views/apps/onboarding/ExamPlanStep.vue (new)
- src/student/full-version/src/views/apps/settings/StudyPlanSettings.vue (new, editable)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Students/StudentPlanConfig.cs (new VO; coordinate with ADR-0012 split)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Students/StudentActor.Commands.cs (SetStudyPlanConfig handler)
- API endpoint + validator (deadline future, weekly budget 1-40h)
- Tests: happy path, boundary, locale-correct date picker he/ar

## Definition of Done
- Student sets exam date + weekly budget during onboarding and edits from settings.
- Values persist and flow into SchedulerInputs at session start.
- RTL correctness: dates/numerics wrapped in `<bdi dir="ltr">`.
- E2E test covers onboarding → settings edit → scheduler consuming values.
- Full Cena.Actors.sln builds cleanly.

## Reporting
complete via: node .agentdb/kimi-queue.js complete <id> --worker kimi-coder --result "<branch>"

title: Live caller for AdaptiveScheduler at session start
queue_body:
## Goal
`AdaptiveScheduler.PrioritizeTopics` is called only from tests. Wire it into LearningSessionActor so every new session requests a priority plan, stores the resulting CompressedPlan session-scoped (ADR-0003), and exposes it via the session API for the student UI's trajectory view.

## Files
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Sessions/LearningSessionActor.cs (call PrioritizeTopics on session start)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Sessions/SessionPlanSnapshot.cs (new VO, session-scoped, NOT persisted to StudentState)
- API endpoint `/api/session/{id}/plan`
- SignalR event `SessionPlanUpdated`
- Tests: plan generated on start; respects DeadlineUtc from prr-148; plan expires with session (arch test: no leak to StudentState/Marten)

## Definition of Done
- New session creates a plan via AdaptiveScheduler using SchedulerInputs from student config.
- Plan is session-scoped — dies with the session, not persisted to profile.
- Student UI can fetch /api/session/{id}/plan; SignalR updates flow.
- Architecture test passes.
- Cost gate: per-session plan generation MUST NOT issue an LLM call (heuristic only); assert via cost-audit test.
- Full Cena.Actors.sln builds cleanly.

## Reporting
complete via: node .agentdb/kimi-queue.js complete <id> --worker kimi-coder --result "<branch>"

title: Mentor/tutor override aggregate for schedule
queue_body:
## Goal
No mentor-override mechanism exists for the schedule today. MethodologyAssignment overrides teaching style, not schedule content. Design + implement a new event-sourced aggregate allowing a teacher/mentor to (a) pin a specific topic for a student's next N sessions, (b) adjust that student's weekly budget, (c) override motivation profile for specific session types. Fully auditable, tenant-scoped.

## Files
- docs/adr/NNNN-teacher-schedule-override.md (new ADR)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Teacher/ScheduleOverride/ (new bounded context)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Teacher/ScheduleOverride/TeacherOverrideAggregate.cs
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Teacher/ScheduleOverride/Events/ (PinTopicRequested_V1, BudgetAdjusted_V1, MotivationProfileOverridden_V1)
- src/actors/Cena.Actors/Sessions/LearningSessionActor.cs (consult override when building SchedulerInputs)
- src/admin/full-version/src/views/apps/teacher/StudentScheduleOverride.vue
- Tests: tenant-scope (institute A teacher can't override institute B student), audit-trail, precedence (override > student input > scheduler default)

## Definition of Done
- ADR accepted.
- Aggregate ships with full event sourcing, tenant-scoped, audited.
- Teacher UI allows pin/unpin/budget-adjust with confirmation.
- Scheduler respects overrides with documented precedence.
- Arch test: no cross-tenant read path.
- AdminActionAuditMiddleware captures every override action.
- Full Cena.Actors.sln builds cleanly.

## Reporting
complete via: node .agentdb/kimi-queue.js complete <id> --worker claude-subagent-teacher-override --result "<branch>"

title: Manual triage — 4 partial-match orphans + 16 partial-match handoffs
queue_body:
## Goal
Manually triage 20 items (4 partial-match orphans + 16 partial-match handoffs) left by the tight-match audit; classify each as merge-into / promote-as-new / fold-into-scanner / false-orphan / defer-to-epic.

## Why human, not agent
Further agent matching produces the same partial-match bucket under different thresholds. Handoff semantics need context.

## Files
- pre-release-review/reviews/audit/tight-match-orphans.md (partial-match rows)
- pre-release-review/reviews/audit/tight-match-handoffs.md (partial-match rows)
- pre-release-review/reviews/triage-partial-matches.md (new — write outcomes)

## Definition of Done
- 20 entries in triage-partial-matches.md with classification + justification
- promote-as-new → new prr-NNN task files; merge-into → absorbed-finding note; fold-into → prr-019/prr-040 scope update; false-orphan → covering task cited
- SYNTHESIS.md + README counts updated if new tasks were promoted

## Reporting
complete via: node .agentdb/kimi-queue.js complete <id> --worker human-architect --result "<triage summary>"
